body.u: After leaving school my first big step in life was to find a job, or make a career for myself. I had no qualifications, but I passed an entry exam for the Royal Navy, as an engineer. This was a wise first step. During this time I had a very nice girlfriend which I love very much. The next move was to marry her which I done when I was twenty, because at this I left the Royal Navy and found a job on civy-street this I found very easy, because I had a good record and a fine trade.

Then came the buying of the house which was very hard because the mortgage was so high. A few years went past and I had a baby boy whose name was Wayne. I am now twenty-three with a nice house, and a beautiful family. I {know?} find leasurepleasure playing rugby, football, tabletennis and other sports.

Rugby I am fairly and at the age of twenty five (two years solid fitness training) I play for the local first team, which means I am winning a few medals every year. By this time my second child is on the way, I am hopeinghoping for a girl.
Three more monthe’smonths go past and I have a baby girl, who we name Francheska.
My parents died about ten years after which left me with no family on my side (I wasn’t close to what family there was on my side).
Wayne was now really getting into his school work. Francheska wasn’t very far behind, they were both going to be brainy kids.
After these exam’s came the wedding’sweddings of my two children, which were very beautiful, there mother cried all the way through the church service.
We are now getting to werewhere we have three grandchildren.
I wouldn’t have changed my life in any way if I had it over again.
